findings: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) exhibit moderate shortcut-like signals indicating class-conditioned structural variation.
These features show high variance ratios and differing unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes, supporting the presence of dataset-specific artifacts.
The moderate shortcut signals suggest a risk of overfitting due to representation-sensitive patterns in backward packet length features.
limitations: No duplication verification was performed to check for exact data duplication artifacts.
